title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot meets with Miss Lemon’s sister, Mrs. Haberdasher (also referred to as Mrs. Hubbard or Mrs. Harbottle), for afternoon tea to discuss the unusual thefts at the dormitory. Mrs. Haberdasher provides a notebook listing the missing items, which include an evening dress shoe, artificial jewelry, a powder box, lipstick, a stethoscope, earrings, a cigarette lighter, old flannel pants, a light bulb, a box of chocolates, a torn scarf, a canvas backpack, boric acid powder, and Epsom salt. Poirot notes the strange randomness of the list, though he finds the stethoscope and a diamond ring particularly significant. The diamond ring, which belonged to a student named Patricia Lean, was previously discovered in a soup bowl belonging to another student, Valerie Hobhouse. Mrs. Haberdasher explains that the ring was Patricia's mother's engagement ring, though its appearance in the soup dish was initially dismissed as a prank.

As Poirot analyzes the victims and the stolen goods, he learns more about the students involved. Patricia Lean is described as a quiet, respectable, and non-wealthy archaeology student who has recently quit smoking. Valerie Hobhouse is a clever, dark-skinned woman with a sharp tongue who works at a beauty salon called "Sabrina Goddess." Poirot observes that while some items might appeal to a vain or cash-strapped individual, the theft of the stethoscope—which belongs to a pleasant young man named Mr. Bateson—suggests a thief capable of pawning professional medical equipment.
